summary.intent: Fix GitHub issue #655 in the moltis repo: honor the `[skills] enabled = false` config flag at runtime so skills are not injected into the LLM prompt when disabled.
summary.outcome: Implemented a `discover_skills_if_enabled` helper in `crates/chat/src/lib.rs` that gates skill discovery on `config.skills.enabled`, replaced all 4 inline `FsSkillDiscoverer` callsites with the helper, added two regression tests, and submitted a PR after passing clippy/fmt/test checks.

repo: The `[skills] enabled` flag lives in `SkillsConfig` at `crates/config/src/schema.rs:1275` and was documented but never enforced at runtime.
All LLM-facing skill discovery callsites live in `crates/chat/src/lib.rs`; non-chat callsites (cli, gateway, httpd, tools, watcher) also bypass the flag but are considered out of scope for this fix.

Two callsites (send path L3712, model-probe path L4957) lack a `persona` in scope and must use `moltis_config::discover_and_load()` directly; two others (L5048, L5165) have `persona.config` already available.
The `moltis_config` crate exposes `set_data_dir` / `clear_data_dir` helpers for overriding the global data directory in tests.
Tests that mutate the global `data_dir` override must be serialized — achieved with a module-level `tokio::sync::Mutex` (needed because guards are held across `.await` points).
The project uses a nightly toolchain (`nightly-2025-11-30`) for fmt and clippy, and a `just lint` recipe for quality gates.
Conventional commit format (`fix(skills):`) triggers automatic changelog generation.

code.finding: New `discover_skills_if_enabled` async helper added here, between `prompt_build_limits_from_config` and `resolve_channel_runtime_context`.
First callsite (main send path): replaced inline FsSkillDiscoverer block with helper; config loaded via `moltis_config::discover_and_load()` since no persona is in scope.
Second callsite (model-probe / supports_tools path): replaced inline discoverer; config also loaded via `discover_and_load()`.
Third callsite (load_prompt_persona_for_session path 1): replaced with helper using already-loaded `persona.config`.
Fourth callsite (load_prompt_persona_for_session path 2): same as above, `replace_all` was used since both blocks were identical.
Two regression tests added at end of test module: one asserts empty result when disabled (even with a planted SKILL.md on disk), one asserts live discovery when enabled.

workflow: Using `replace_all: true` on the Edit tool is effective when two callsites have identical code blocks.
Clippy flagged `tokio::sync::Mutex` type annotation as unused-qualifications when `Mutex` was already imported — using the unqualified `Mutex<()>` resolved it.
Running `cargo check` before the full clippy pass catches compile errors faster.
The beads issue was created and claimed before implementation, then closed after the PR was submitted — standard workflow for this repo.

friction: Clippy flagged `std::sync::Mutex` usage in async tests (guard held across `.await`) — required switching to `tokio::sync::Mutex`.
The `tokio::sync::Mutex` fully-qualified type annotation then triggered an unused-qualifications lint, requiring a second edit to use the unqualified form.
Multiple Read passes on `lib.rs` were needed to locate exact line numbers for callsites because the file shifted after inserting the helper.
open_items: Non-chat callsites that bypass `[skills] enabled`: `crates/cli/src/main.rs:495`, `crates/gateway/src/services.rs:236` and `863`, `crates/gateway/src/server.rs:3887`, `crates/skills/src/watcher.rs:115`, `crates/tools/src/skill_tools.rs:368`, `crates/httpd/src/server.rs:2492` — these don't inject into LLM prompts but still ignore the flag.
A follow-up issue should be filed to expand the `enabled` gate to non-chat callsites for full coverage.
Optional: add a prompt-builder snapshot test asserting the `<available_skills>` block is absent when skills are disabled.
